summary: Iran war escalation with Strait of Hormuz closure creates stagflation shock - oil +35% weekly while jobs crash -92k. Defensive positioning required while maintaining selective AI infrastructure exposure through crisis.
what_changed: Critical escalation: Iran war reaches maximum intensity with Trump demanding unconditional surrender
Supply shock: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed (8 vs 120 ships/day normal), oil hitting $90+
Economic weakness: Jobs crashed -92k vs +50k expected, unemployment rising to 4.4%
Fed policy confusion: Stagflation setup prevents clear monetary response
Market stress: VIX spike to 29.49 signals crisis mode across all asset classes

actions_to_consider.rationale: 15% position too large in crisis - reduce to 8-10% given -22k unrealized loss and energy sector volatility
Increase cash equivalent to 60-65% given crisis conditions and data outages preventing proper analysis
Short duration position working (+$1.3k) but stagflation creates complex Fed dynamics - monitor closely
Short position profitable (+$767) but tech volatility high - consider partial profit taking
AI infrastructure thesis intact but monitor correlation with broader tech selloff

what_to_watch: Strait of Hormuz shipping data - any signs of reopening or further deterioration
Fed communications on stagflation response - rate policy in conflicted environment
Trump administration Iran policy escalation or de-escalation signals
Next employment data to confirm trend vs one-off weakness
Data sourcing issues resolution - critical for bond desk analysis
BE position stop loss at $129.16 - currently at $135.19 and vulnerable

top_conviction_trade.thesis: Crisis demands maximum liquidity preservation. Data outages prevent proper analysis across asset classes. Cash is the only position with full conviction until geopolitical shock stabilizes and data flows resume.
